programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison becomes hooked on repeats of a 1960s TV show while ill, and learns one of the actors was convicted of murdering his wife. However, a series of strange dreams allow her to reinvestigate the case

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy intelligence officer, and the choice of murder weapon leads the agents to believe he was killed by a colleague. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The FBI's domestic terrorist task force, including operational psychologist Nate Getz, steps in to help the team when a former marine is suspected of creating a chemical bomb. Crime drama, starring Peter Cambor and Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee feels the pressure as the team investigates a double murder that appears to be inspired by his still unpublished new novel. His sense of guilt is compounded by the realisation that one of his colleagues could become the killer's third target. American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service, starring Sean Murray and Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: A jogger is found dead, and with no-one around to see how she died, the woman's fitness tracker appears to be the only 'witness' to the crime. Making use of their cyber expertise, Avery Ryan and her team crack into the device and retrace the victim's steps for the entire night leading up to her untimely demise. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The teams fly to Los Angeles from Hawaii in pursuit of suspect Jarrod Taylor, before tracking him down at his hotel, where he claims he is being paid by an immunologist who is hoping to reintroduce smallpox to restore nature's balance. Later, the agents make a shocking discovery when they find a bulk order for children's t-shirts. Concluding part of the crossover episode with Hawaii Five-0

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gillian Taylforth joins the cast as Nikki Wright, a Sergeant from the Barton Street station who finds herself caught up in an armed stand-off along with her Sun Hill colleagues. James Tennant's life continues to fall apart as he is arrested for drink driving and assault

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lola is shocked to find Abra is a patient at the psychiatric hospital where she has recently been given a job, and plans to reunite him with Kyla. Starring Sharon D Clarke and Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Following the horrific train crash, Merlin keeps a constant vigil at Anna's bedside, but it soon becomes clear that she won't recover from her injuries. Meanwhile, Jim warns Nikki not to attach herself emotionally to orphan Paul, Comfort is plagued by flashbacks from the derailment and Harry discusses whether he should cease his course of medication having finally got over the tragic loss of his wife

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey investigates an assault on a prostitute and ends up offering her a room for the night, ignoring Jack's advice to avoid emotional involvement. Later in the day, he bumps into ex-girlfriend Mia and finally manages to talk to her about his rape, which has left him unable to form close bonds with people. Yvonne makes progress in the case against GP Andrew Wilder and Dan's girlfriend dumps him in humiliating circumstances

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square is in a state of shock after Debbie's death. David tries to make plans for Bianca's birthday, and Ruth and Mark make plans for a baby. Grant proposes to Sharon

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon struggles to decide whether or not she will marry Grant

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Olivia is still feeling unwell and disconcerted by James's continued distance, while Elizabeth feels put out by talk at the dinner table, and Sarah tries to separate her social status from the Blighs by putting Roy in between her and the wealthy family

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A chemical spillage outside the Black Swan plunges Cardale into a state of emergency, and it's up to Andrew and Sam to save lives. Over at the Beeches, Joanna attends to some of the injured, but is terrified of risking her unborn baby's health by inhaling the toxic fumes. Rural drama, starring Gary Mavers, Joseph Millson and Susannah Corbett

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried takes control when a cantankerous colt proves too much for James and Tristan. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star in the rural drama

programme.title: French Fields
programme.desc: William suffers his first brush with French law enforcers after Hester invites his new boss over for dinner. Comedy, starring Julia McKenzie and Anton Rodgers

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Marion's intimate knowledge of pharmacology exposes Harvey's bogus illness. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene is imprisoned by communist leader Louise, who plans to use him as her love slave, while Mimi, Edith and Yvette are convinced he is dead and decide to hold a seance

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley and Barry devise another money-making plan to sell hot dogs to weary hikers and travellers, while Clegg, Truly and Tom fondly remember Compo and imagine how Nora Batty's arrival in Australia will cause misery for the folks Down Under. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Kenneth Branagh returns to his Bafta-winning role of the Swedish detective in three adaptations of stories by Henning Mankell. Wallander moves into a new home with his girlfriend, only for their dreams of domestic bliss to be shattered when they discover a skeleton in the garden. He must find the killer if he has any hopes of getting his life settled again - and is surprised to discover the body was buried more recently than he first thought. Saskia Reeves and Sarah Smart co-star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After Sandra's departure, the detectives are anxious to meet their new boss, and DCI Sasha Miller wastes no time getting cracking with her first Ucos case, the murder of an Italian immigrant on a west London allotment 25 years earlier. The ritualistic style of the killing puts one of the other gardeners in the frame when it's revealed he is a practising druid. However, as the investigation continues, Gerry becomes suspicious of Sasha - why is she kissing a senior officer? Tamzin Outhwaite joins the cast as DCI Miller, with Dennis Waterman, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The agents find themselves forced to rely on a British traitor from the Cold War era when they infiltrate a Russian firm suspected of stealing secrets from GCHQ. Struggling to cope with recent events, Adam insists on running the operation - but it is soon clear he has taken on too much. George Baker (Inspector Wexford) guest stars in the high-octane drama, with Rupert Penry-Jones and Peter Firth

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Intrigue and frustration are the order of the day as Anna and Karen come to terms with the possibility that a patient may be suffering domestic abuse. programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe helps the owners of a local Chinese takeaway whose pride and takings are steadily being eroded by racist attacks, while the Great British Grub competition looks set to be a roaring success. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe agrees to cook for a reluctant witness in a police safe house, and comes under threat from both a ruthless villain and an unscrupulous copper. Meanwhile, Henderson is alarmed by an unwelcome reminder of a past indiscretion. Comedy drama, starring Richard Griffiths

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Humiliated that Sir Henry Simmerson has allowed the French to make off with an English standard, Sharpe vows to make amends by capturing the most jealously guarded battlefield prize of them all - a French eagle emblem touched by the hand of Napoleon himself. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley and Brian Cox star

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: An alleged sexual assault requires delicate handling. The boat where the attack took place belongs to a friend of the Commissioner, forcing the detectives to go undercover at a fancy dress party as they attempt to arrest their suspect. With only circumstantial evidence and a victim of unreliable character, Ray surprises his colleagues by proving he has a gentler side, gaining the confidence of a young witness

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene comes up against a socialist feminist group when a man's death he initially treated as suicide becomes the subject of a murder inquiry. Chris uses the arrest of every member as an opportunity to impress Shaz with his knowledge of women's issues, while Ray dates a communist. An intriguing code found by Alex and deciphered by the desk sergeant leads the team closer to the truth

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Bacchus and Gently investigate a consignment of stolen passports, but the case takes an unexpected turn when a woman linked to the crime is found dead on the seashore with her mixed-race baby lying next to her. As the duo follow a lead on the murder victim's ex-boyfriend, they encounter racial prejudice and are drawn into an underground world of gang wars within the Arab community. Primeval's Andrew-Lee Potts guest stars

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike and the team are reacquainted with some familiar faces when investigating a suspicious suicide at the Brokenwood Women's Prison. For Mike this one is personal - he knew the victim Corina Doyle from his days prior to Brokenwood, and with her passing, new information surfaces about an old crime

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The driver of the ladies' motorcar rally team is found dead in her roadster, leaving Phryne struggling to convince Jack that her friend's death was not an accident. Australian crime drama, starring Essie Davis and Nathan Page

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: During a heatwave in Brighton, the Lyell team is summoned to a waste dump where a body part has been found. The search is on to recover the other dismembered remains and piece together the victim's body, which is covered in intricate Japanese tattoos. Away from the lab, Nikki has some news that she desperately wants to share with Matt, but must wait until he visits from America

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Jim and the team investigate when a dismembered body is found under the floorboards of a Glasgow house. programme.desc: Australian drama about a hedonistic heart surgeon, Dr Hugh Knight, who is forced to work as a rural GP in his hometown of Whyhope. Starring Rodger Corser

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor unwittingly saves the life of a brutal East End villain, who begins terrorising patients and staff alike. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave

programme.title: Bramwell

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Slaters receive an unpleasant warning message through the letterbox, and while Kush assumes it is from his creditors, Stacey is sure Ruby has sent it. Determined to give her old friend a piece of her mind, she sets off for the club - unaware she is walking into danger. Ritchie warns Phil that his criminal record will go against him in a custody battle, Peter notices that Bobby is still frantically cleaning his hands and Habiba gives Iqra an ultimatum. Sharon gets a shock when Denny's things are returned from the boat accident

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, inviting audience members from Sedgefield to ask questions of former Education Secretary Nicky Morgan, Shadow Treasury Secretary Bridget Phillipson, Nobel Prize-winning US economist Joseph Stiglitz, Children's Commissioner for England Anne Longfield, and Stephen Fitzpatrick, Chief Executive of OVO Energy

programme.title: Newscast

programme.title: A Question of Sport

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband-and-wife team ain to add sparkle to a three-bedroom flat in Epsom in Surrey, a niece and aunt team up to refurbish a house in Wolverhampton and a semi-retired man acquires a bungalow in Ashton in Cornwall. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell talk to the developers and hear their plans to see what they want to do with their homes bought at auction

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Matt Forde and journalist and political commentator Ayesha Hazarika to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Nora competes with Edmund for their grandmother's affection. Wally creates an Instagram account to impress his crush

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Grandma tells Nora the story of how she met her husband in the style of a Korean drama

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sara Pascoe. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on. She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall recycling centre on a mission to save three unwanted items, rescuing a well-used table, a retro chair and rusty gate rods from the skip

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special current affairs edition of the general knowledge quiz. Featuring John Pienaar, Ayesha Hazarika, Iain Dale, Jacqui Smith, Camilla Tominey, Rachel Johnson, Martin Bell and Michael Fabricant

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the chosen six bands make their debuts, competing to impress and performing head to head for the very first time, with only five bands making it through to the next round

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to review Thursday's game between Philadelphia Eagles and New York Giants, plus a look ahead to the other fixtures in week seven

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Witley, Surrey, Sarah Moore calls on blacksmith Kev Paxton to revamp a pair of metal bed ends, while upholsterer Ray Clarke gives an old armchair a complete redesign. For her own transformation, Sarah has a chest of drawers carcass to repurpose

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement agents Ben Dirom and Myles Whitworth visit a car dealership to get a refund for a customer who bought an unroadworthy car, but are subjected to a barrage of abuse. In Peckham, sheriffs Luke Peacock and Grant Bailey shut down a cosmetics retailer where the owner has failed to pay his debts

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Roo Irvine, trader Danny Sebastian and upcycling blacksmith Kevin Paxton head to the Scottish Borders to rummage through its antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets in the hope of finding items they can each turn into a healthy profit. When the day is done and they've got their items, they have just three weeks to turn them into cash

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is the annual cricket match between Weld and their sworn enemies Stafford, and George has been roped into the team as a ringer despite being banned from cricket in Australia

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The contest, hosted by Joe Lycett, reaches its final as the last three contestants take on a trio of evening wear challenges before the winner is announced. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young begin by asking the sewers to create a double-breasted waistcoat. Next is the Transformation challenge, as the trio repurpose net curtains into exquisite, red carpet-ready, pieces of evening wear. Lastly, for the Made to Measure challenge, the sewers attempt to construct and fit glamorous strapless evening gowns for their models, this time hand-picked for friends and family

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Five experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, with David Harper excited by an early device for watching films, while David Fletcher and James Lewis reminisce about Rupert the Bear books and Dinky toys. Paul Martin enjoys a ride in a tank on historic former military camp

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Mike Bushell, Aisling Bea, Sunetra Sarker and Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Alpaca farmers Ian and Caroline and former circus ringmaster-turned-farmer Drew compete with their alpacas for coveted red rosettes at the Royal Three Counties Show

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary talks to award-winning film-maker and Turner Prize-winning artist Steve McQueen, discussing his views on growing up in London and his new BBC One anthology series Small Axe. Set from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s, each film tells a different story involving racism and discrimination in London's West Indian community

programme.title: Wild Shetland - Scotland's Viking Frontier
programme.desc: Ewan McGregor narrates a portrait of wildlife on the Shetland Islands, tracing the course of a breeding season from the depths of winter to high summer. Cameras follow puffins, diving gannets and storm petrels nesting in an ancient Pictish tower, a mother otter protecting her young twin cubs, and capture a pod of orcas hunting seals of the island's rocky coasts

programme.title: The Trump Show
programme.desc: Key players including Rudy Giuliani, Stormy Daniels, Steve Bannon and Ambassador John Bolton shed light on Trump's second year, as he faces challenges from his past

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Angela Barnes, Rhys James, Nigel Ng and Athena Kugblenu. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A team of transplant surgeons perform a double lung transplant operation, a procedure that must be carried out against the clock to avoid tissue deterioration. They successfully remove one diseased lung and replace it with a healthy one. However, just as they cut off the blood supply to the second lung, the transplanted organ stops working. At Addenbrooke's, a neurosurgeon removes a bundle of abnormal blood vessels sitting near the top of a patient's spinal cord

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of ordinary people fighting for truth and justice following acts of terror. The first one featured is a hostage crisis in Syria that culminated in seven beheadings by a British executioner, later dubbed Jihadi John, while the second looks at the far-right attack in Christchurch, New Zealand, in which 51 people were shot dead in the city's mosques

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A police officer shot at close range is in danger of losing his sight, and a speeding motorcyclist is pursued through London

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of the programme, Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the best and worst buys the reds and blues have bought over two decades

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, including a dressing table and mirror, and vintage suitcase

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Sonnaz Nooranvary takes on the repair of a wooden barber's chair that once took pride of place in an Essex shop, while Steve Fletcher and Dominic Chinea restore a clock. Silversmith Brenton West is on hand to fix a precious metal box that is tarnished, tattered and coming apart at the seams. Made of a delicate metal called antimony, it poses a real challenge for Brenton, who isn't helped when Steve warns him of his own youthful experience trying to repair antimony

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Antiques expert Mark Stacey, wheeler-dealer Tony Wong and upcycler Zoe Pocock head to Exeter to find some bargains they can turn into profit for Children in Need. The three experts try to outdo each other by scouring the city's antiques shops, scrap yards and flea markets in search of any items they can convert to cash. When the day is done and the time is up, they then get just three weeks to turn their goodies into gold

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: Everyone is talking about George and Fiona's clinch after the cricket match, and events come to a head in the street. Comedy drama, starring Erik Thomson

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: Joe Lycett welcomes twelve more contestants to the amateur sewer competition. First up, the sewers are challenged to create seemingly straightforward wardrobe staples, and for their pattern challenge, the judges ask them to make a wrap skirt. Finally, the contestants attempt to make their first made-to-measure garment, a tea dress

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The final episode focuses on life on the longest mountain range in the world, the Andes, home to astonishing hidden worlds, and extraordinary animals and people. Cameras follow a female puma and her three cubs as they hunt in the mountains of the frozen south, and the descendants of the Inca gather in an ancient ceremony to build a bridge made from woven grass

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In this episode from Rochester Cathedral in Kent, antiques expert Elizabeth Talbot finds a rare Peter Rabbit book and Adam Partridge discovers a Concorde necklace that's one of a kind. Presenter Paul Martin meet the men of Chatham Dockyard, who kept the Royal Navy afloat

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Sports reporter Mike Bushell, comedian Aisling Bea, actress Sunetra Sarker and football pundit Dion Dublin go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games. This edition sees the week's overall winner crowned

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Northern Ireland's first charcuterie maker Alistair is joined by Kenny and Jennifer - whose menagerie of animals are stars of the silver screen - as they aim to sell their home-reared chorizo and meat at the Irish Game Fair. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: Simply Nigella
programme.desc: Food writer Nigella Lawson rustles up more of her favourite recipes, this time with the emphasis on cosy and comforting mood. For breakfast she makes a tray of dairy-free chai muffins, while there's a low-key lunch of Asian-flavoured short ribs - a perfect make-ahead, tender beef stew. Inspired by her visits to America's west coast, an informal supper for friends features Nigella's home-made, oven-baked fish tacos, which involve a bit of DIY at the table, followed by a salted chocolate tart for pudding

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Host Sara Cox is joined by contributors Rebecca Front, Sophie Ward, Ade Adepitan and Bill Bailey to discuss this week's book group picks - Fifty-Fifty by Steve Cavanagh and Sophie Ward's Love and Other Thought Experiments

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French Revolution

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don gives tips on how to look after wildlife in the months ahead, shows how to look after and protect tender garden plants and plans ahead for next year's harvest of fruit. Joe Swift finds out what it takes to look after a historic garden with a collection of heritage fruit trees in Hampshire, and Arit Anderson meets Fergus Garrett - head gardener at Great Dixter - to discover how the planting and garden management have increased biodiversity. Plus, the Staffordshire couple with a particular fondness for Japanese maples

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools chats to guest Celeste - the BBC's Sound of 2020 - about her musical journey so far. The soulful singer made her debut on Later a year ago, shortly after the release of her hauntingly beautiful breakthrough hit Strange, and her loves and influences are illustrated by a diverse selection of clips from the show's archive. This week's performances also offer introductions to two artists tipped for success in 2021 - Wigan band the Lathums and singer-songwriter Joesef

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories, with Faisal Islam

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Part two of two. Infuriated that President Roslin has usurped his command by ordering Starbuck to steal the captured Raider, Adama decides that his only option is to storm Colonial One with marines and terminate her presidency. Starbuck reaches Caprica and finds the Arrow of Apollo in a museum, but she has to go through a copy of Number Six to get it. The fighters clash with the Cylon ship and Roslin remains determined to find a way of reaching Earth. Mary McDonnell stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: With Adama out of commission, Tigh takes over command of the Galactica. Gaeta tries to execute an emergency jump but enters the wrong co-ordinates and the ship is separated from the civilian craft. The truth about Boomer is confirmed, and she is detained in prison, although she manages to break free and absconds on Starbuck's Cylon raider

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Colin Jackson investigates the hidden world of eating disorders in British sport, speaking to athletes from amateur to elite level and discussing his own problems with food when he was a 110m hurdler. He asks why those involved in sport are far more likely to have an eating disorder than the rest of the population, and talks to UK Sport chair Katherine Grainger about what the authorities should be doing to tackle the problem.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: David Jenkins, a world-leading surgeon at Royal Papworth, performs one of the most demanding operations possible, draining a patient's body of blood to remove clots in her lungs. At Addenbrooke's hospital, consultant orthopaedic surgeon Andrew Carrothers is also pushing at the boundaries of medical science with a complex operation that he has devised called the Harrington Plus. Frustrated by the lack of options for patients whose pelvis and hip bones have been badly weakened by cancer, Andrew added a crucial stage to an existing operation to enable cancer patients to walk again

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine footage of an octopus that thinks it is Houdini, a stoat that hypnotises its prey, and a massive lake in America that disappeared

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Featuring a Korean rocket festival and solar-powered supertrees in Singapore

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Adam attempts some dog training techniques with Henry, while vet Rory is in studio to show how to make your own doggy treats at home. And we hear how BP fans have been celebrating Black History Month.

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia and Wallace book a holiday to the Costa Del Generica

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia must act fast as Wallace becomes possessed by an evil toaster

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve's on a quest to find the deadliest prehistoric aquatic monster

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the second of two programmes in which pooches and pussies are put through a number of tests to see which family pet comes out on top. There are five more rounds, concentrating on which animal is easiest to train, communication, which is best at helping reduce people's stress, independence and the big question - do cats and dogs really love their owners? On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris meets a Border Collie which can recognise more than 150 words and Liz meets a cat capable of choosing its own TV channels

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon host the revival of the programme in which radio-controlled robots created by teams of amateur engineers battle it out in an arena. The machines have to make it through an initial group stage, then head-to-head rounds before the heat final. The first show features students from Reading and a family team from Caithness, as well as a memorable robot from the original series

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry

programme.title: A Star Is Born (1954)
programme.desc: A Hollywood movie star with a drinking problem is captivated by an aspiring singer's performance. He falls in love with her and helps her become a star, but as her career blossoms, his falls into ruin. Musical drama, starring Judy Garland, James Mason, Jack Carson and Charles Bickford. Directed by George Cukor, with songs by Harold Arlen and Ira Gershwin

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is joined by Claire Rawle and James Lewis at St Albans Cathedral, where items include some historic coins and a 1970s robot

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Fraserburgh crew race to save a stricken fishing boat without a working rudder in a force-eight gale. On Loch Ness, a yacht becomes trapped on a weir, and one wrong move by the lifeboat crew could send it over the edge. On the north Devon coast, the Minehead crew comes to the aid of a puppy that has swallowed a fishing hook

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin recalls the making of his third travel series Full Circle, a journey around the Pacific Rim which took him to Russia, China, Vietnam, Australia and New Zealand and North and South America. This series saw Michael adopting a more investigative role than in previous odysseys, particularly when encountering the drug wars and poverty that plagued Colombia. With contributions from David Attenborough and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: Jamie Crawford takes to the skies to explore Scotland's cities, coasts and countryside, using aerial images to uncover fascinating stories from Scotland's past. On the island of Canna, Jamie scrambles down a cliff-face to uncover an ancient Christian building, and in the north-east he takes a plunge in a fabulous but freezing outdoor swimming pool

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon plan to stage a re-enactment of the St George and the dragon story as part of the Wings for Victory fundraising campaign, but discover Hodges and his fellow ARP wardens have had the same idea. Vintage comedy first shown in 1977, starring Arthur Lowe

programme.title: Maggi Hambling: Making Love with the Paint
programme.desc: Painter and sculptor Maggi Hambling allows cameras into her rural Suffolk studio for the first time, talking to film-maker Randall Wright about her life and career in a film to mark her 75th birthday. Formidable and funny, Maggi recalls her artistic liberation during her student days, a memorable meeting with Francis Bacon in Soho, and falling in love with his muse Henrietta Moraes, a woman Maggi continues to draw from memory following her death. With insights from Maggi's partner and fellow artist Tory Lawrence, colleague Sarah Lucas and art writer James Cahill

programme.title: Apostasy (2017)
programme.desc: A Jehovah's Witness is forced to shun her own sister because of a religious contravention. As the separation drags on, she starts to question the meaning of God's love. Drama, starring Siobhan Finneran, Robert Emms, Bronwyn James

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. Barry White's deep baritone also put both black and white audiences 'in the mood'. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary crew follows the daily lives of four vampires sharing a house in New Zealand as they prepare for an annual gathering of the undead. A new member of the household introduces the bloodsuckers to modern life, but his failure to follow the laws of their kind brings his flatmates nothing but trouble. Horror comedy, directed by and starring Jemaine Clement and Taika Waititi

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's mining heritage takes Fred from Cornwall to Scotland, where he visits sites of lead, slate, tin and coal extraction. Along the way, the machine-mad former steeplejack has a chance to man a winding engine and descends a mine shaft while strapped to the top of the cage

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Snowy peaks point toward the heavens as American painter Bob Ross surrounds a scene in a happy gathering of landscape finery

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon (1949)
programme.desc: A cavalry officer on the brink of retirement takes drastic steps to prevent an impending war with the Arapaho people, stirred up by double-dealing agents and Custer's recent defeat. After a series of skirmishes and minor victories, he decides the best solution is to ride into the Arapaho camp and negotiate a truce. John Ford Western, starring John Wayne, Joanne Dru, John Agar and Ben Johnson

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits

programme.title: Glen Campbell: The Rhinestone Cowboy
programme.desc: An insight into the life of country music star Glen Campbell, documenting his remarkable journey from an impoverished childhood to worldwide fame and success as a guitarist and singer with records including Wichita Lineman and Rhinestone Cowboy. Featuring contributions by family, friends and colleagues including Jimmy Webb, Micky Dolenz and Bob Harris

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: Neil Oliver presents the drama-documentary telling the tale of the bloody 17th century Civil War. When the Scottish Parliament, led by the Chief of Clan Campbell, declares war on Charles I, Clans loyal to the King rise up in rebellion. At the heart of this epic struggle lies an ancient feud between the Campbells and the MacDonalds, and the Royalist Clans use the war to wreak bloody vengeance on their rivals in an epic conflict that threatens the stability of the newly emerging British state

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak focuses on the art of the new cities of the east coast of America, with their futuristic skylines and lofty skyscrapers. He explores the squalid boxing rings painted by George Bellows, Reginald Mash's decadent away days on Coney Island and the escape into theosophy and abstraction mounted by Thomas Wilfred. He ends by looking at the harsh immigrant experience of Ellis Island and the profound impact that rootlessness had on the art of Mark Rothko.

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Snowy peaks point toward the heavens as American painter Bob Ross surrounds a scene in a happy gathering of landscape finery

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Industrial Age
programme.desc: A tour of Britain's mining heritage takes Fred from Cornwall to Scotland, where he visits sites of lead, slate, tin and coal extraction. Along the way, the machine-mad former steeplejack has a chance to man a winding engine and descends a mine shaft while strapped to the top of the cage

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and musician fronts a three-part series on the movie musical, from Hollywood to China and the USSR, and from Snow White and Gene Kelly to Grease and La La Land. In this opening episode, Brand argues that the movie musical was the most important form of cinema from the advent of the age of sound. Beginning with the very first film musical, 1929's Broadway Melody, he looks at the huge and lasting impact of the genre and, in his trademark analysis of songs at the piano, analyses some of the most important numbers in this first 'Golden Age'

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on February 2, with performances by artists including Sybil, Eurythmics, Skid Row, Cher, Sinead O'Connor and the House of Love

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on February 8, with performances by artists including Phil Collins, the Beloved, Janet Jackson, Del Amitri and Beats International featuring Lindy Layton

programme.title: Count Basie Through His Own Eyes
programme.desc: Hitherto, little has been known about Count Basie's private and family life, but here director Jeremy Marre offers up a treasure trove of home movies and photo albums that show the bandleader and pianist's remarkable relationship with his wife Catherine - whose pioneering support for African-American causes placed her at the side of Martin Luther King - and disabled daughter Diane. Through Basie's intimate footage and letters, this film reveals the passions and ambitions that inspired the world-famous musician. Contributors incluide his friends Quincy Jones and Annie Ross

programme.title: Blue Note Records: Beyond the Notes
programme.desc: When German-Jewish refugees Alfred Lion and Francis Wolff started Blue Note in 1939 in New York, the duo allowed artists complete freedom and encouraged them to compose new music. Their visionary and uncompromising approach led to releases that not only revolutionized jazz, but also left an indelible imprint on art and music, including hip-hop. This documentary delves behind the scenes to look at the pioneering label that gave voice to some of the finest jazz artists of the 20th and 21st centuries. Featuring rare archival interviews with Blue Note musicians

programme.title: Jazz Piano Gold
programme.desc: Archive footage of performances from 1964 to 2009 by artists including Count Basie, Thelonious Monk, Oscar Peterson, Abdullah Ibrahim, Stan Tracey, Jacques Loussier, Duke Ellington, Return to Forever and Herbie Hancock. Clips are taken from shows such as Jazz 625 and Later with Jools Holland

programme.title: Hot Chocolate at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC performances and rare interview extracts celebrating British pop group Hot Chocolate and in particular its lead soloist Errol Brown. The band's top 10 hits from the 1970s and early 1980s are showcased, alongside rarely seen early performances and cult fan favourites

programme.title: Count Basie Through His Own Eyes
programme.desc: Hitherto, little has been known about Count Basie's private and family life, but here director Jeremy Marre offers up a treasure trove of home movies and photo albums that show the bandleader and pianist's remarkable relationship with his wife Catherine - whose pioneering support for African-American causes placed her at the side of Martin Luther King - and disabled daughter Diane. Through Basie's intimate footage and letters, this film reveals the passions and ambitions that inspired the world-famous musician. Contributors incluide his friends Quincy Jones and Annie Ross

programme.title: The Sound of Movie Musicals with Neil Brand
programme.desc: The composer and musician fronts a three-part series on the movie musical, from Hollywood to China and the USSR, and from Snow White and Gene Kelly to Grease and La La Land. In this opening episode, Brand argues that the movie musical was the most important form of cinema from the advent of the age of sound. Beginning with the very first film musical, 1929's Broadway Melody, he looks at the huge and lasting impact of the genre and, in his trademark analysis of songs at the piano, analyses some of the most important numbers in this first 'Golden Age'

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto investigates the truth behind the sinking of Venice and discusses the likely long-term effect of a current exodus of inhabitants leaving to find jobs elsewhere. He explains how 19th-century British artists and writers, including Turner and Dickens, wove a romantic myth around the city, and describes his family's connection to one sexually promiscuous figure

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: Travelling through Vietnam, Michael stops off along the way to witness the changing face of Hanoi before boarding the Reunification Express for Saigon - an emotionally charged journey which throws up many memories of the Vietnam War. In the Philippines, he watches a psychic surgeon at work, but ends the programme on a lighter note by helping judge the Miss Bella Pacifica contest

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Rolf Larsen, a respected detective on the Copenhagen police force, investigates the case of a missing child, but his life is brutally upended when his own baby daughter goes missing. Five years after the event, a new lead emerges when a serious flaw is uncovered in the police's DNA database. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: A stolen car in Thornby results in Rolf meeting young police assistant Neel and the detective is reluctantly ordered by their joint boss to test the car thief's DNA with his old colleagues in Copenhagen. In Danish, English, Polish and French

programme.desc: Jas decides to follow in her dad's footsteps and try out for the junior hockey team. Mr Salford was a legendary hockey goalie in his time at the school. When the try-outs are cancelled due to the coach not being available, Mr Salford steps in and offers his services as coach. Mrs Griggs is horrified - she could do without the chaos he inevitably brings with him - but he won't take no for an answer. Jas and her father are really superstitious and when things start going wrong Jas thinks she's jinxed, and then that she's passed on the jinx to her dad. When she's not picked as goalie for the team Jas is actually relieved - it must mean she's still jinxed and hasn't passed on the jinx to her dad. Eventually Jas does need to play in goal, but when she saves a crucial penalty she and her dad are delighted to realise they are not jinxed after all, just accident-prone! programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Zionne, Isaiah and Mandi are young black teenagers who have all experienced racism growing up in the UK. Mandi has been called racist names, Isaiah is often followed round shops by security guards, and Zionne feels she will always have to work harder than everyone else to succeed. In the first of a two-part special, they speak out about what life is really like growing up black in the UK and why things urgently need to change. After the death of George Floyd and the nationwide protests against racism, the teenagers know that this is their time to be heard. As well as opening up about their own experiences, they speak to their friends and their parents. They decide to create a manifesto to list the big things they feel need to change in society to make life better for black kids like them.

Lily is Martha’s disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go on a bizarre quest to find the most unusual, strange and amazing places to visit in the UK. This time the team take part in Ely's eel-throwing competition, journey to Land's End in Cornwall where they learn how to make a true Cornish pasty, explore Luton's top attractions with Game Show Man and mystified locals, visit Northern Ireland to discover the connection between Star Wars porgs and Rathlin Island puffins and pay tribute to Dolly Parton on a taxi tour of London, while Holly H reveals some weird facts about the UK’s capital city. Ed is joined on this amazing and offbeat road trip by Sam and Mark, Lindsey Russell, Naomi Wilkinson, Annabelle Davis, Max and Harvey, Joe Tasker, The Mandeville Sisters and Holly H - bet you didn’t know this stuff was in the UK, but it turns up All Over The Place!

programme.title: Billionaire Boy ()
programme.desc: Family comedy, by David Walliams and Kevin Cecil. Len Spud and his 12-year-old son Joe become billionaires when Len invents a new toilet roll. Len soon starts dating fortysomething hand model Sapphire Diamond, while the butler helps a neglected and unhappy Joe transfer to the local comprehensive where he conceals the facts of his wealth. However, when Len turns up at the school with his son's forgotten homework, the cat is soon out of the bag. John Thomson stars as the inventor, alongside Walliams, Catherine Tate and Rebecca Front, and featuring Warwick Davis and Bryn Terfel as themselves

programme.desc: Oti teaches us how to move lika a train

programme.title: Cbeebies The Ugly Duckling
programme.desc: A telling of the Hans Christian Anderson story about an ugly duckling's search to find out who she really is, featuring the Northern Ballet

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin surprises Robert and insists on giving his best robot friend a day to remember

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels 100 million years back into prehistoric times to meet spinosaurus, a massive meat-eating dinosaur even bigger than a T-Rex. On his adventure he rides a speedy ouranosaurus and takes a dip in a prehistoric river

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: It's Waffle's last day at the Brooklyn-Bells', but he still finds time to upset neighbour Mrs Hobbs. Simon hears Waffle speak - will he finally agree that the family can keep the special dog? With the voice of Rufus Hound

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly dresses up as a fairy godmother and makes Suki and Ruby's wishes come true. But Magnus's wish to build a shiny robot doesn't go as smoothly

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Mummy asks the twins to sort out their old toys. She wants them to give away the ones they do not use any more before they move house

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When Sam's dreamcatcher breaks she blames David and eventually Sam's feelings about David's arrival start to surface

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat: Rockpool Tales
programme.desc: Old Jack tells a story to Salty dog about the day Little Sue gets a new toy boat and the Rockpool friends have a race. Bernard Cribbins stars

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley goes to Shropshire to find out how quinoa is harvested, and then makes a tasty salad. Meanwhile, JB Gill discovers what turf is and how it is grown

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: With Ferne Corrigan and Rory Crawford

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Bobble spots a lonely star in the night sky, but even Groan Up cannot reach it. The Olobobs invent Skyder who carries them up to space, and the little star is lonely no more

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble makes breakfast for Tumble Ted. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are collecting eggs for their breakfast

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 106 million years to the time of the Leaellynasaura - a dinosaur that lived in Australia. Along the way he battles with a deadly polar allosaurus

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits the barbers to see how hairclippers work and learns how ballet shoes are made

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter invites Jeremy Fisher along for a garden raid, the melodramatic frog gives him more than he bargained for

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Ubercorn and Glitch have to team up when they get stuck at the most remote place on Earth.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran busts a groove when she teaches JoJo the moves to a traditional Saint Lucian dance. JoJo finds that learning a dance can take time, until she realises she can put her own twist on it.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon recalls the first time he met Waffle as a pup and the pair share memories of the Wonder Dog becoming part of the Brooklyn-Bell family

programme.title: My Pet and Me
programme.desc: Rory visits Jess and her sheep dog Mo, and watches the pair as they herd sheep, before having a go himself. He also helps clean out Mo's kennel and ensure she has food and water

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the docks to get on board an old sailing ship.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: One Po loves reading so much, he's walled himself into his home with piles of books

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: Cheetah cries because she is no longer as fast as she used to be, and cannot beat the wind in a race

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is finding out about things that go up and down. She shows us how a lift works, when it carries people up and down between floors of a building. With the help of an engineer and her special cameras, Maddie show us how the gears and weights are controlled by a computer. Next, it's time for lift-off as Maddie heads to a space museum. We see how a spacesuit is made from lots of different layers of special materials to keep astronauts safe when they are working in space, and Maddie uses her special thermal camera to see how the suit keeps astronauts warm.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is excited to show Gran Gran around the city farm - it's been a few months since she was last there, and she can't wait to see Melanie the chick again. But when they arrive at the chicken enclosure, they can't find the little fluffy chick - where could she be?

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: It is Hogmanay on the island, and the little girl awaits the arrival of her parents and new baby sister. But a storm has delayed their ferry, and they might miss the celebrations

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The Ribbon sisters invite Sarah and her feathered friend to the park for their favourite seasonal event

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Lola finds a toy rabbit near school and is worried about it. Charlie puts it on the school wall, but when it is still there later in the day, Lola insists they take it home

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: It's tidying up day and Tee's friend Lily the Tiger comes round to play her favourite game; Surprise! But can they surprise Mo?

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Everyone has an exciting ride on the Pinky Ponk when the Ding Dong alarm sounds

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Suranne Jones reads Before I Wake Up, by Britta Teckentrup

programme.desc: Consumers test and score a dog-training tool, vegan nail varnish and a T-shirt for children that can be coloured-in, while the makers watch on. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple looking for a home in the Wye Valley, and also examines the challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel in the River Wye

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Slaters receive an unpleasant warning message through the letterbox, and while Kush assumes it is from his creditors, Stacey is sure Ruby has sent it. Determined to give her old friend a piece of her mind, she sets off for the club - unaware she is walking into danger. Ritchie warns Phil that his criminal record will go against him in a custody battle, Peter notices that Bobby is still frantically cleaning his hands and Habiba gives Iqra an ultimatum. Sharon gets a shock when Denny's things are returned from the boat accident

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: The deaths of three generations of one family in June 1999, a brutal mass murder that shocked the close-knit community of Clydach, near Swansea. Wyre Davies reports on new evidence

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, inviting audience members from Sedgefield to ask questions of former Education Secretary Nicky Morgan, Shadow Treasury Secretary Bridget Phillipson, Nobel Prize-winning US economist Joseph Stiglitz, Children's Commissioner for England Anne Longfield, and Stephen Fitzpatrick, Chief Executive of OVO Energy

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Fergus Walsh discuss the latest on the Covid-19 crisis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: The scammers setting up fake websites to lure unsuspecting customers into paying for goods that don't exist. Plus, the wedding woes of brides-to-be who have lost out thanks to the pandemic. Presented by Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of a terraced house in Ramsbottom in Bury, a former shop in Gravesend in Kent and a house in Newcastle-under-Lyme

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Thirty dogs and two racoons are found kept in filthy conditions, some of them locked in old cars. There's no water, food or in some cases ventilation. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete, as series veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference
