programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: New products include a unisex t-shirt that repels water-based liquids and kills odour-causing bacteria and a Champagne bottle opener. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple fulfil a long-held dream of moving to the Scottish countryside of Ayrshire. Alongside a selection of the region's best properties that a budget of £450,000 can buy, Nicki also pays a visit to the 18th-century Dumfries House, home to one of the finest collections of furniture in the world

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves a drop-leaf table, an old wingback chair and a collection of metal baskets from the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by Stephanie and daughter Mackenzie's collection of 1940s costume drawings. Other items stimulating interest are an Edwardian safe, a cluster of unusual figurines and a very old Corpus Christi. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling great Chris Hoy, former England defender Stuart Pearce, world champion swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and Great Britain 400m star Perri Shakes-Drayton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil works on a plan to prove that Ellie is in no fit state to look after a child. Callum helps by accessing information on Ellie - only to be caught in the act by DI Thompson. Meanwhile, Denise finally makes up her mind, and Ian checks on Sharon after seeing her upset over the return of Denny's belongings

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock, comedian Phil Wang, actor Jason Watkins and EastEnders star Adam Woodyatt, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Matt Forde and journalist and political commentator Ayesha Hazarika to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian faces the challenge of another proper job as he takes over the running of a hotel in the Pembrokeshire seaside town of Tenby. Rhod has stayed in many hotels while on tour, but cleaning, cooking breakfast and dealing with guests' complaints is not what he'd registered for in this hotel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of ditching her mod cons to embrace nature and help the environment. She tries giving up destructive habits like driving short distances and using plastic packaging to go camping in the Welsh Hills, where she meets a family whose members have embraced the delights of living off-grid

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Nora competes with Edmund for their grandmother's affection. Wally creates an Instagram account to impress his crush

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Grandma tells Nora the story of how she met her husband in the style of a Korean drama

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sara Pascoe. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on. She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple search for a new home in South Wales, big enough to house their huge collection of superhero memorabilia

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Katie Keat attempts to bring a wooden swivel seat back to its best, while Sarah has a collection of cutlery to find a use for

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: In the quiet fishing village of Porthgain, Pembrokeshire, Derek Brockway meets local seaman Captain Brian Thomas, who spent weeks in a coma after collapsing while out walking. The pair explore the area's industrial past and soak in the stunning sea views. Then, at RSPB Conwy, the presenter meets Nicola Cockburn who has been blind since birth, to find out what considerations are involved when she goes out walking and how she manages to indulge her passion of bird-watching

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special current affairs edition of the general knowledge quiz. Featuring John Pienaar, Ayesha Hazarika, Iain Dale, Jacqui Smith, Camilla Tominey, Rachel Johnson, Martin Bell and Michael Fabricant

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the chosen six bands make their debuts, competing to impress and performing head to head for the very first time, with only five bands making it through to the next round

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to review Thursday's game between Philadelphia Eagles and New York Giants, plus a look ahead to the other fixtures in week seven

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are intrigued by Stephanie and daughter Mackenzie's collection of 1940s costume drawings. Other items stimulating interest are an Edwardian safe, a cluster of unusual figurines and a very old Corpus Christi. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Harry Judd round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith investigates an uncertain future for upland sheep producers, while Anne Lundon discovers Scotland's Tree of the Year. Arlene Stuart heads to Rannoch in Perthshire to meet an inspiring man who is determined to access the countryside despite his disability

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil works on a plan to prove that Ellie is in no fit state to look after a child. Callum helps by accessing information on Ellie - only to be caught in the act by DI Thompson. Meanwhile, Denise finally makes up her mind, and Ian checks on Sharon after seeing her upset over the return of Denny's belongings

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Cycling great Chris Hoy, former England defender Stuart Pearce, world champion swimmer Keri-Anne Payne and Great Britain 400m star Perri Shakes-Drayton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Jennifer Saunders hosts the satirical quiz, inviting comedian and writer Matt Forde and journalist and political commentator Ayesha Hazarika to join regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton as they poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host offers a wry look at some trending news stories before welcoming a galaxy of stars - both in the studio and online - for a chat. Rock legend Bruce Springsteen promotes his new album Letter to You, Kristin Scott Thomas talks about the new Netflix adaptation of Rebecca and Oscar-winner Matthew McConaughey discusses his memoir Greenlights. Comedian Mawaan Rizwan promotes the new season of Taskmaster, while actor Stephen Mangan talks about Sky Arts' Portrait Artist of the Year. Plus, musical guest Sam Smith chats and performs current single Diamonds

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Nora competes with Edmund for their grandmother's affection. Wally creates an Instagram account to impress his crush

programme.title: Awkwafina Is Nora from Queens
programme.desc: Comedy about a girl navigating young adulthood in outer-borough New York City. Grandma tells Nora the story of how she met her husband in the style of a Korean drama

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer, Craig Fowler, Shaughan McGuigan and Joel Sked present a sideways look at Scottish football, dissecting of ins and outs of the week's action, with a variety of features covering the full spectrum from the giants to the minnows. The team is joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Freddy Forster and Anna Haugh, and special guest Sara Pascoe. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares some of her favourite recipes for biscuits and small bites to snack on. She kicks things off with her deceptively simple but extremely beautiful Italian-inspired amaretti biscuits, before demonstrating how to make deep-fried chicken doughnuts. Finally, Nadiya shares her recipe for chocolate-dipped florentines

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Leicester City at Emirates Stadium

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the Wye Valley, where he helps an entrepreneurial couple with a £650,000 budget find a dream home which allows them to build a rural business. Along the way he visits the UK's first solar aquaponics greenhouse and discovers how 21st-century technology there could be the sustainable solution to growing enough food to solve the world's food shortage

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall recycling centre on a mission to save three unwanted items, rescuing a well-used table, a retro chair and rusty gate rods from the skip

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people to the room, with a lazy Susan, Italian jewellery, a cigar case, and a ship's wheel on offer to the five professional dealers

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special current affairs edition of the general knowledge quiz. Featuring John Pienaar, Ayesha Hazarika, Iain Dale, Jacqui Smith, Camilla Tominey, Rachel Johnson, Martin Bell and Michael Fabricant

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The girl group search for the next global singing sensation, with the winning band joining them on their next UK tour. Tonight, the chosen six bands make their debuts, competing to impress and performing head to head for the very first time, with only five bands making it through to the next round

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the 12 celebrity and professional dancer pairings take to the dancefloor for their debut performances. Each of the performances will be critiqued and scored by Strictly judges Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and Motsi Mabuse, but the competitors will escape the public vote until the following weekend

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Brothers Gabriel and Daniel from Devon face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Manchester United v Chelsea at Old Trafford. West Ham were thrashed 5-0 here by the Citizens last term, but had enjoyed some good results in recent weeks. United overcame the Blues the first three times they met last term, but Frank Lampard's men gained revenge with a 3-1 win in the semi-finals of the FA Cup. Plus, Liverpool v Sheffield United, Aston Villa v Leeds United and Fulham v Crystal Palace

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the latest Premiership fixtures, featuring Kilmarnock v Hibernian, Motherwell v Ross County, St Johnstone v Dundee Utd and St Mirren v Hamilton Academical. Jonathan Sutherland presents, with analysis by Chris Iwelumo and Steven Naismith

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to review Thursday's game between Philadelphia Eagles and New York Giants, plus a look ahead to the other fixtures in week seven

programme.title: The Uninvited (2009)
programme.desc: A teenager is left traumatised by her mother's death in a fire, and is committed to a mental institution after trying to kill herself. A year later, she is released and reunited with her sister, but they both begin to suspect their new stepmother may have been responsible for the fire. Thriller, starring Emily Browning, Arielle Kebbel, David Strathairn and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford investigate why energy bills are leaving customers at their wits' end, and fraud on a big-name internet shopping site

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Cumbria, Pembrokeshire and Derbyshire, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement agents shut down a filthy takeaway infested with mice, and investigate a salmonella outbreak that struck down more than 50 guests at a wedding. Narrated by Harry Hepple

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Near Peterhead, an elderly patient who has difficulty moving needs to be transferred to hospital. Ambulance crew Ricky and Luan use their skills to help him into the ambulance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate 20 years of the programme, Eric Knowles takes a look back at some of the best and worst buys the reds and blues have bought over two decades

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers test and score a dog-training tool, vegan nail varnish and a T-shirt for children that can be coloured-in, while the makers watch on. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple looking for a home in the Wye Valley, and also examines the challenges of sustainably sourcing and restocking the European eel in the River Wye

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, including a dressing table and mirror, and vintage suitcase

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers are offered to chance to bid on a vintage table hockey game, an early-20th-century glass chandelier and antique sketch books that belonged to the artist Henry Thomson

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Presented by Alex Jones and Gethin Jones

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Slaters receive an unpleasant warning message through the letterbox, and while Kush assumes it is from his creditors, Stacey is sure Ruby has sent it. Determined to give her old friend a piece of her mind, she sets off for the club - unaware she is walking into danger. Ritchie warns Phil that his criminal record will go against him in a custody battle, Peter notices that Bobby is still frantically cleaning his hands and Habiba gives Iqra an ultimatum. Sharon gets a shock when Denny's things are returned from the boat accident

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Two siblings seek dietary advice for their parents, who have let healthy eating fall by the wayside after a stressful year dealing with bereavement and caring for an elderly mother. Gregg Wallace demonstrates that a satisfying meal can be made easily from fresh ingredients without their frequently used cooking sauces, while Chris Bavin suggests healthy homemade alternatives to takeaways

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: A look back at memorable advertising tasks - a staple of the show and famously Lord Sugar's favourite of all the assignments. There's a chance to relive classic clangers such as Jet Pop - the most inappropriately named airline in history, and Elizabeth making a modern car advert in a medieval village. Plus, a lesson in exactly how it should have been done when Carina used her loaf to make a bread commercial. Watching along are Lord Sugar, Baroness Brady and Claude Littner. They'll reveal the ads worth tuning in for, and those that had them switching off. Narrated by Tom Allen

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, inviting audience members from Sedgefield to ask questions of former Education Secretary Nicky Morgan, Shadow Treasury Secretary Bridget Phillipson, Nobel Prize-winning US economist Joseph Stiglitz, Children's Commissioner for England Anne Longfield, and Stephen Fitzpatrick, Chief Executive of OVO Energy

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Adam Fleming, Laura Kuenssberg and Fergus Walsh discuss the latest on the Covid-19 crisis

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With double Olympic taekwondo champion Jade Jones, England rugby union winger Ugo Monye, gold medal-winning hockey star Sam Quek and former Manchester United defender Gary Pallister. Sue Barker asks the questions, with team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Live
programme.desc: The scammers setting up fake websites to lure unsuspecting customers into paying for goods that don't exist. Plus, the wedding woes of brides-to-be who have lost out thanks to the pandemic. Presented by Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband-and-wife team ain to add sparkle to a three-bedroom flat in Epsom in Surrey, a niece and aunt team up to refurbish a house in Wolverhampton and a semi-retired man acquires a bungalow in Ashton in Cornwall. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell talk to the developers and hear their plans to see what they want to do with their homes bought at auction

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers discover an illegal puppy farm in Cannock, putting profit over care. Thirty dogs and two racoons are found kept in filthy conditions, some of them locked in old cars. There's no water, food or in some cases ventilation. Plus, a rogue kitchen trader swindling customers out of thousands of pounds

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: In Paisley, a young cyclist has his bike ride cut short after a tangle with his chain leaves him with a gash in his leg. He's full of bravado, but badly injured. Nearby, an elderly lady with breathing problems needs to be treated and carefully transferred to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of experts compete, as series veterans Thomas Forrester, Philip Serrell and Kate Bliss take on more recent additions Tim Weeks, Danny Sebastian and Roo Irvine. As a team they each have one hour and £300 to buys three items to take to auction, but to turn up the heat they will also be shopping on their own in a relay. Presented by Natasha Raskin Sharp

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.desc: New products include a unisex t-shirt that repels water-based liquids and kills odour-causing bacteria and a Champagne bottle opener. Presented by Lucy Alexander

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple fulfil a long-held dream of moving to the Scottish countryside of Ayrshire. programme.title: Air an Smùid (Steaming)
programme.desc: A team of puffer enthusiasts battles to save the rusty, corroded VIC 27 from the scrap heap. On her rusty bow she bears the name Vital Spark, a throwback to her glory days as star of the BBC's 1994 series The Para Handy Tales. The team members look on nervously as she's carefully winched up the purpose built slipway. This is the first time in almost 30 years she's been out of the water, and at last they can assess the condition of her hull prior to ferrying her by canal to Crinan where her restoration begins

programme.title: Crodh Gàidhealach an Àird Bhàin: The Highland Cattle of Ardbhan

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: This summer, America has seen one of the most bitterly fought presidential election campaigns in living memory. As the President himself recovers from Coronavirus, voters reflect on the pandemic that has left America with the highest number of Covid-19-related deaths in the world, and the impact this has had on the economy. A summer of protests over racial justice has seemingly deepened the divide between Democrats and Republicans, and an increasingly ominous rhetoric around the validity of the election result could threaten the democratic process itself. So as Americans go to the polls, have they had enough their maverick leader or are they ready to vote him in for a second term? Can Democratic challenger and former Vice President Joe Biden make the case for a calmer, more traditional presidency that could begin to heal some of America's festering wounds? ITV's Senior International Correspondent John Irvine travels down the Mississippi River, from Minneapolis to St Louis, talking to A

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is keen to unite as a family, Gabby takes charge at work, and fans the flames of Leyla's paranoia

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The financial expert is joined by Angellica Bell for another round of advice on family finances, jobs and consumer rights, including Martin's topical briefing and tips on making money go further

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Annalise and boyfriend Jamie, and father-and-son Rhys and Adam try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £1,000,000. Game show, hosted by Phillip Schofield

Taking in the popular West Highland way, it's just a quick boat ride to the idyllic island of Inchailloch, a jewel in the loch's crown, where stunning views over the immense mass of water can be enjoyed

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show

programme.title: Sorry, I Didn't Know

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Father-and-son pairing Adam and Rhys see their journey inside the Cube reach a dramatic conclusion. Hosted by Phillip Schofield

programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha presents recipe ideas for the week ahead based on different cuisines from around the world, beginning with the tastes of Italy. programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: An author re-visits the hotel, not realising that the kids cleaned up after a recent party and accidentally got rid of the ghost author who really writes his books!

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Teen Titans Go takes a comedic look at the superheroes' lives. Viewers will finally get the opportunity to watch what life is like for the super teens.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: Guests dropping into the kitchen include Katie Melua and chefs Dipna Anand and Stephen Terry. James meets up with Grace Dent to go on a culinary tour of Birmingham, gives a masterclass in jam-making and prepares another recipe on the banks of the River Test

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares meals for a lazy Sunday, joined by Gyles Brandreth, Henry Firth, Ian Theasby and Candice Brown. On the menu are sweetcorn fritters served with avocado, tomato and black bean salsa and poached eggs, custard tarts, bloody Mary with polenta mozzarella sticks, and rosemary and anchovy slow roasted lamb with roasted roots, while Gyles contributes his take on the fish finger sandwich

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v Italy (Kick-off 3.30pm). Coverage of both teams' fourth match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium, as the competition resumes after a break of more than seven months. Ireland had won both their home fixtures, overcoming Scotland and Wales, but they did lose their last match against England at Twickenham. However, they will be expecting to get back to winning ways today as the Italians have not won a Six Nations contest since 2015. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Rory Best, Brian O'Driscoll and Maggie Alphonsi, commentary by Miles Harrison, Gordon D'Arcy and Danielle Waterman, and reports from Gabriel Clarke

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking Rob Beckett, Ruth Jones and Jordan Banjo to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Jacqui Oatley, Dev Griffin, Fallon Sherrock and Johnny Vegas pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Million Pound Cube
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield hosts a celebrity edition of the game show in which Stacey Dooley and Kevin Clifton try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £1,000,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: Jonathan is joined in the studio by guests comedian Russell Howard, classical singer Katherine Jenkins and Line of Duty star Daniel Mays. Rapper Dizzee Rascal will be chatting about his career and performing live in the studio

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: World heavyweight boxing champion Anthony Joshua and comedians Kevin Bridges and Jon Richardson join team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh for physical games and head-to-head quizzes. Regular panellist Romesh Ranganathan lines up alongside Lampard, with Seann Walsh serving as resident score-keeper

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: This summer, America has seen one of the most bitterly fought presidential election campaigns in living memory. As the President himself recovers from Coronavirus, voters reflect on the pandemic that has left America with the highest number of Covid-19-related deaths in the world, and the impact this has had on the economy. A summer of protests over racial justice has seemingly deepened the divide between Democrats and Republicans, and an increasingly ominous rhetoric around the validity of the election result could threaten the democratic process itself. So as Americans go to the polls, have they had enough their maverick leader or are they ready to vote him in for a second term? Can Democratic challenger and former Vice President Joe Biden make the case for a calmer, more traditional presidency that could begin to heal some of America's festering wounds? ITV's Senior International Correspondent John Irvine travels down the Mississippi River, from Minneapolis to St Louis, talking to A

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: The CIA teaches Stan about 'rubbernecking', the art of checking out other women away from the gaze of their wives. programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland discovers his parents have been selling all his childhood possessions in an effort to reclaim money they lost to a conman. Desperate to set things right, he enlists the aid of his friends to get revenge on the swindler

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is released from hospital following his heart attack, prompting his friends and family to shower him with love and affection. However, Zoey is not so sure he deserves all the attention. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois reads a new book on the delights of decluttering, so she challenges the rest of the Griffins to throw anything out of the house that does not spark joy

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A year after Jeff was abducted by aliens from Roger's home planet, Hayley meets a new man in the form of millionaire Matt Davis. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins provide DVD commentary on a recent episode and reveal never-before-told drama between Peter and Lois

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve helps Stan fix a problem with his office computer, but feels slighted by his father's colleagues when they claim he isn't 'CIA material'. Meanwhile, committed vegetarian Hayley accidentally eats meat, and realises she still loves the taste

programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon spots local coal merchant Arthur Bolton sharing a kiss with another woman and quickly gives the man's wife the bad news. However, the cheat is found murdered the next day and the former policeman becomes the prime suspect, leaving PC Walker to lead an investigation to clear his name. Peggy fails to back a winner when she buys a greyhound that refuses to run, and Bellamy's love life gets a much-needed boost

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Steve's engagement party gets underway. Alex makes advances towards Linda, and Biff rushes off to find her when Kim tells him she has left with Alex

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken is surprised when Denise turns up with Daniel. Vera insists the Armstrongs move back into the Rovers permanently. Stephen shocks Mike by cancelling his contract with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mike cannot believe Stephen would cancel the contract over Don. Curly tells Andy that nothing happened between him and Anne. Denise tells Ken he could have regular access to Daniel

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: It becomes clear to the detectives that the four men have conspired to come up with a false account of what took place the night Hayley disappeared. Jake is assigned to track down Tim Finch's ex-wife to hear her version of events - and uncovers disturbing information about Chris Lowe's past. On a personal front, Sunny is offered the opportunity to rekindle his past marriage, and Cassie worries about her father's failing memory

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.desc: Rob lands himself in trouble when he spends the night with Helen, getting Geoff to cover his shift - and matters grow complicated after it's revealed that the doctor's estranged husband made the complaint. programme.desc: Biff frantically tries to revive Linda until an ambulance arrives. Kim tells Tara she caught Alex taking cocaine. Biff receives some tragic news at the hospital

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim tells Sophie she feels guilty about Linda's death. Biff confronts Kim and warns he is out to get her. The police reveal the results of Linda's post mortem to Biff and Ned

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Anne tells Curly she is glad Andy has gone, Tricia lords it over Samantha at the Rovers, and Jim pushes Bill to make up with Maureen

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud wonders why Maureen has cooled towards Bill. Fiona is shocked when she goes for a date with Alan at an Italian restaurant. Alma tells Mike how Stephen humiliated her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Morwenna's parents Jack and Tara pay a surprise visit, having been working as missionaries in Kenya. However, the reunion is tinged with sadness when it turns out that Morwenna's mother has liver cancer, and has decided against treatment because of her faith. Louisa's first online call for her new course does not go as planned - especially when Martin tells the lecturer exactly what he thinks - and Joe investigates a break-in at the school where nothing has been stolen. Martin Clunes stars in the comedy drama, with Jessica Ransom, Caroline Catz, John Marquez, Kevin Doyle and Rebecca Lacey

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: James, Tim and Chris are exposed as having lied about their whereabouts on the night Hayley died. Pete reveals that he saw Eliot climbing into the holiday home through a window on New Year's Day, while leaves James struggling with the possibility that his son may have killed Hayley. Fran cracks a code in the dead girl's diary, suggesting that she met a local drug dealer on the night she disappeared

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: The team discovers a number of items that appear to belong to Hayley in Tim's house, providing the damning evidence needed to convict him of her murder. He tries to protest his innocence, claiming he bought them for his daughters at a fete. His story seems to be backed up when Jessica and her mother reveal that none of the items found belong to Hayley, leaving the investigation back at square one. Meanwhile, a new discovery has a shocking impact on the detectives, but proves especially devastating for Cassie

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Carry On Doctor (1967)
programme.desc: A popular doctor is sacked after a misunderstanding ruins his reputation, prompting the furious patients to stage a revolt against the luckless medic's strait-laced superiors. Meanwhile, the hospital head has his own problems after becoming the unlikely romantic target of a strict matron. Comedy, with Frankie Howerd, Jim Dale, Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Hattie Jacques

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mary investigates the noises in Eileen's loft, David dreads taking sole responsibility for Shona, and Gary convinces Ray that he could be an asset to him

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An FBI agent crosses swords with Poirot while trying to retrieve the American Navy's top-secret plans for a new submarine, which have been stolen by a glamorous nightclub singer with a sideline in espionage. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.desc: The flatmates crave a bit of summer warmth, and Mrs Roper's sunlamp mysteriously disappears. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: It becomes clear to the detectives that the four men have conspired to come up with a false account of what took place the night Hayley disappeared. Jake is assigned to track down Tim Finch's ex-wife to hear her version of events - and uncovers disturbing information about Chris Lowe's past. On a personal front, Sunny is offered the opportunity to rekindle his past marriage, and Cassie worries about her father's failing memory

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rob lands himself in trouble when he spends the night with Helen, getting Geoff to cover his shift - and matters grow complicated after it's revealed that the doctor's estranged husband made the complaint. programme.desc: Biff frantically tries to revive Linda until an ambulance arrives. Kim tells Tara she caught Alex taking cocaine. Biff receives some tragic news at the hospital

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud wonders why Maureen has cooled towards Bill. Fiona is shocked when she goes for a date with Alan at an Italian restaurant. Alma tells Mike how Stephen humiliated her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Morwenna's parents Jack and Tara pay a surprise visit, having been working as missionaries in Kenya. However, the reunion is tinged with sadness when it turns out that Morwenna's mother has liver cancer, and has decided against treatment because of her faith. Louisa's first online call for her new course does not go as planned - especially when Martin tells the lecturer exactly what he thinks - and Joe investigates a break-in at the school where nothing has been stolen. Martin Clunes stars in the comedy drama, with Jessica Ransom, Caroline Catz, John Marquez, Kevin Doyle and Rebecca Lacey

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: James, Tim and Chris are exposed as having lied about their whereabouts on the night Hayley died. Pete reveals that he saw Eliot climbing into the holiday home through a window on New Year's Day, while leaves James struggling with the possibility that his son may have killed Hayley. Fran cracks a code in the dead girl's diary, suggesting that she met a local drug dealer on the night she disappeared

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: The team discovers a number of items that appear to belong to Hayley in Tim's house, providing the damning evidence needed to convict him of her murder. He tries to protest his innocence, claiming he bought them for his daughters at a fete. His story seems to be backed up when Jessica and her mother reveal that none of the items found belong to Hayley, leaving the investigation back at square one. Meanwhile, a new discovery has a shocking impact on the detectives, but proves especially devastating for Cassie

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A book dealer is found drowned, having seemingly committed suicide in despair at having sunk all his cash in a local inventor's revolutionary new transport system - but the discovery of a second body makes it clear that there is a murderer at large. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Kate Buffery and Richard Todd

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Sergeant Troy is promoted to Inspector, but before he can consider his future he has to help Barnaby investigate the collapse of a tunnel which has exposed a collection of ancient skeletons - and more recent remains among the bones suggest links to a nearby double shooting. Murder mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Cherie Lunghi and David Bradley

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual harvest fair and the daredevil riders of the Wall of Death arrive in the Midsomer village of Whitcombe Mallet. But when the owner of an equestrian centre is trampled by his horse, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson, along with the expertise of forensic pathologist Kam Karimore, must unravel a complex feud from the past. However, nothing is as it seems, and they are in a race against time as they work to solve the case. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee, Manjinder Virk and Fiona Dolman

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Georgia (Kick-off 7.30pm). Coverage of the autumn international fixture at BT Murrayfield. After disappointing defeats to Ireland and England in the Six Nations back in February, the Scots were much improved against the French in Edinburgh as two tries from winger Sean Maitland helped secure a 28-17 win in their final match before the Covid-19 pandemic struck. With their final Six Nations game against Wales on the horizon, coach Gregor Townsend will use this match to fine-tune his team. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Sir Ian McGeechan and Jim Hamilton, and commentary from Simon Ward and Scott Hastings

programme.title: Live International Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Georgia (Kick-off 7.30pm). Coverage of the autumn international fixture at BT Murrayfield. After disappointing defeats to Ireland and England in the Six Nations back in February, the Scots were much improved against the French in Edinburgh as two tries from winger Sean Maitland helped secure a 28-17 win in their final match before the Covid-19 pandemic struck. With their final Six Nations game against Wales on the horizon, coach Gregor Townsend will use this match to fine-tune his team. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Sir Ian McGeechan and Jim Hamilton, and commentary from Simon Ward and Scott Hastings

Sitting alongside the elegant Victorian and Edwardian properties in south-west London, this three-up, three-down is ugly from the outside and even worse on the inside, with dark, dated and badly designed spaces. Architects Katerina Dionysopoulou and Billy Mavropoulos are brought in to make most radical transformation on the programme to date - but will their wildly ambitious poured-concrete extension prove too innovative for the owners?

It remains to be seen what Daisy May Cooper and Mawaan Rizan will be doing - but it's unlikely to be dignified

programme.title: Trump's Coronavirus Catastrophe
programme.desc: Matt Frei investigates the president's policies, actions and decisions during the pandemic, which has cost more than 200,000 American lives. programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Morland Sanders investigates the American food that could be coming to Britain soon as part of a post-Brexit trade deal. with exclusive lab results revealing levels of dangerous bacteria on US supermarket meat. He looks at the widespread use of pesticides and hormones currently banned in the UK, and uncovers animal welfare concerns and declining hygiene standards in American food production, as well as fears the deal could harm the British farming industry

programme.desc: The presenter remembers some of the most memorable adventures he has had in his beloved transit van. Guy salvaged the supposedly written-off vehicle after a collision with a car transporter, and transformed it with the aid of custom engineers. He has entered it in the Nevada Open Road Challenge, tested it to its limits on Bonneville Salt Flats, and broken a world record at the Nurburgring in Germany

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britain's Buried Secrets

Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: Moonlight (2016)
programme.desc: A young African-American man in a rough Miami neighbourhood grapples with his identity and sexuality while experiencing the everyday struggles of childhood, adolescence and burgeoning adulthood. He finds support from a drug dealer and his kindly girlfriend, who can see he is being neglected and offer him sanctuary. Barry Jenkins' Oscar-winning drama, starring Mahershala Ali, Naomie Harris, Alex Hibbert, Ashton Sanders and Trevante Rhodes

programme.title: QE2: The World's Most Luxurious Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary following life aboard the most famous ocean liner in the world, now a floating hotel in Dubai. Valentine's Day sees two big events happening on board - a lavish brunch for 450 people in the main dining room, followed by a romantic opera gala dinner out on deck. With guests paying top dollar for both, expectations are huge. However, a late delivery threatens to derail the brunch preparations and - adding to the presage of doom - a storm spotted on the horizon seems set to put the kibosh on the open-air element of the gathering

programme.title: The Queen Mother
programme.desc: Documentary exploring pivotal moments in the queen mother's life, starting with the tumultuous events of 1936. She began the year as Duchess of York, but following the abdication of her brother-in-law Edward VIII, her husband George was forced to take on the throne, leaving her unexpectedly thrust into the role of queen and a vital source of support for the unprepared new monarch

programme.title: The Story of the Songs
